title: International Paper Reports Mixed Q4 Results
text: International Paper Co (NYSE: IP) reported a fourth-quarter FY21 sales growth of 14.5% year-on-year, to $5.09 billion, beating the analyst consensus of $5.07 billion. Net sales from Industrial Packaging rose 12.8% Y/Y, Global Cellulose Fibres grew 19.7%, and Corporate and Inter-segment increased 62.8%.

title: International Paper (IP) Q4 Earnings Miss, Sales Top Estimates
text: International Paper's (IP) Q4 results improve year over year, driven by higher demand. The ongoing momentum in demand is expected to aid results in 2022 amid ongoing supply chain challenges.

title: International Paper (IP) Q4 Earnings Lag Estimates
text: International Paper (IP) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -10.34% and 1.75%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2021. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: 7 Undervalued Stocks That Won't Stay That Way for Long

title: Why International Paper (IP) Might Surprise This Earnings Season
text: International Paper (IP) is seeing favorable earnings estimate revision activity and has a positive Zacks Earnings ESP heading into earnings season.

title: International Paper (IP) to Post Q4 Earnings: What's in Store?
text: International Paper's (IP) Q4 results are likely to reflect solid demand for packaging and pulp products. High costs and the impact of the unplanned outage at the Prattville mill might have dented some of these gains.

title: International Paper: Remains Undervalued, A 'Buy' Here
text: In this article, I will revisit International Paper and provide some ideas and guidance as to what I see for the company here. This is a fundamentally sound paper business that has given excellent returns if bought at the right price.

title: 5 Long-Term Payers Announce Dividends
text: Apogee Enterprises Inc. ( APOG , Financial), Procter & Gamble Co ( PG , Financial), International Paper Co ( IP , Financial), Assurant Inc. ( AIZ , Financial) and Ally Financial Inc. ( ALLY , Financial) all recently announced their next dividends.
